title: Glenn Hall (rugby league)

|url-status=dead }} }} '''Glenn Hall''' (born 21 March 1981) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], the [[Sydney Roosters]], the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]], with which he won the [[2008 NRL Grand Final|2008 NRL premiership]] and [[2009 World Club Challenge]] with and the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[National Rugby League]], while also had a stint in the [[Super League]] with the [[Bradford Bulls]]. He primarily played as a {{rlp|sr}} and {{rlp|pr}}. ==Background== Born and raised in [[Sydney]], New South Wales, Hall played his junior football in the [[City of Bankstown|Bankstown]] area for St Christopher's, Panania. ==Playing career== Hall joined his local club, the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], as a teenager and played in their back-to-back [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] premiership-winning teams in 1999 and 2000. He started 2001 in Jersey Flegg, before being elevated to the Bulldogs' [[NSW Cup|First Division]] side mid-season.{{cite web|url=https://thebulldogs.com.au/sportal.php?page=player&id=649 |title=Glenn Hall |website=thebulldogs.com.au |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150405092701/https://thebulldogs.com.au/sportal.php?page=player&id=649 |archive-date=5 April 2015}} ===2002–2003=== In round 12 of the [[2002 NRL season]], Hall made his [[NRL]] début for Canterbury-Bankstown. In Round 15, he scored two tries, his first in the [[NRL]], in the club's 46–6 victory over the [[Canberra Raiders]]. He finished the [[2002 NRL season]] playing in the Bulldogs premiership-winning [[NSW Cup|Premier League]] side. In 2003, he played 5 more games for the club before signing with the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] for the [[2004 NRL season]]. While at Canterbury-Bankstown, Hall played alongside future [[North Queensland Cowboys]] teammate [[Johnathan Thurston]]. ===2004–2005=== In 2004, Hall played a then career-high 16 [[NRL]] games for South Sydney, starting half of his games at prop. In 2005, he only managed 3 games for the club, before signing with arch rivals the [[Sydney Roosters]] for 2006. ===2006=== Hall endured another disappointing season in 2006, only playing 2 games for the [[Sydney Roosters|Roosters]], before leaving the club and joining his fourth club, the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]]. Hall played in the 2006 [[NSW Cup]] grand final for [[Newtown Jets|Newtown]], who were the [[Sydney Roosters]] feeder club at the time, against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]]. Newtown would lose the grand final 20–19 at [[Stadium Australia]].{{cite web|url=https://www.newtownjets.com/pastSeasons/2007-news/2006GrandFinal.php/|title=2006 Grand Final – Newtown Jets|website=newtownjets.com|access-date=7 May 2021|archive-date=7 May 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210507183424/https://www.newtownjets.com/pastSeasons/2007-news/2006GrandFinal.php/|url-status=live}} [[File:GlennHall.jpg|thumb|right|Hall at the celebration of the winning of the 2008 NRL Grand Final]] ===2007–2009=== [[File:Glenn Hall.JPG|thumb|right|100px|Hall with the [[Manly Sea Eagles]]]] In 2007, Hall became a regular member of Manly's first grade team, playing 23 games before he injured his shoulder late in the regular season, and missed Manly's finals campaign, including the grand final defeat by the [[Melbourne Storm]].{{cite news|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/league/nrl-premiership/hall-unlucky-to-miss-grand-final/story-e6frf3ru-1111114500234|title=Hall unlucky to miss grand final|last=Balym|first=Todd|date=25 September 2007|work=[[Fox Sports (Australia)]]|access-date=14 October 2011}} In 2008, Hall played 20 games and scored 6 tries for Manly-Warringah. He started in the second-row in Manly's record-breaking 40-0 [[2008 NRL season|NRL Grand Final]] victory over [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]].{{cite news|url=http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=17097|title=Manly Sea Eagles – 2008 Centenary Premiers|last=Mitchell|first=Matt|date=5 October 2008|work=League Unlimited|access-date=14 October 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120405153103/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=17097|archive-date=5 April 2012|url-status=dead}} In February 2009, Hall was a member of the Manly side that won the [[2009 World Club Challenge]] against the [[Leeds Rhinos]].{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/bradford/8078552.stm|title=Bulls sign Hall from Sea Eagles|date=2 June 2009|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|access-date=14 October 2011|archive-date=3 February 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110203014541/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/bradford/8078552.stm|url-status=live}} In his last season for Manly, he played 22 games and scored 3 tries. ===2010=== Hall moved to England to join the [[Bradford Bulls]] for the 2010 [[Super League]] season. He left the club by mutual consent after a season in which he found himself dropped to the Bulls' reserve team.{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_5359016,00.html|title=Hall heading to Bradford|date=2 June 2009|publisher=[[Sky Sports]]|access-date=14 October 2011|archive-date=6 June 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090606090235/http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_5359016,00.html|url-status=live}} ===2011–2015=== In January 2011, Hall signed a two-year contract to play for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]]. He was named in the North Queensland leadership group and was also one of their vice-captains. That season, Hall played 23 games for the club and was a member of the team's finals match against the Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles, starting the game in the second row. He averaged 24 tackles and 70 metres a game.{{cite news|url=http://www.brisbanetimes.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/glenn-hall-signs-on-with-cowboys-20110119-19vjp.html|title=Glenn Hall signs on with Cowboys|date=19 January 2011|work=brisbanetimes.com.au|access-date=14 October 2011|archive-date=10 March 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110310061602/http://www.brisbanetimes.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/glenn-hall-signs-on-with-cowboys-20110119-19vjp.html|url-status=live}} On 25 May 2012, Hall re-signed with the North Queensland club until the end of the [[2013 NRL season]].{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/hall-resigns-cowboys-5976/|title=Glenn Hall re-signs with the Cowboys|date=25 May 2012|access-date=25 May 2015|archive-date=25 May 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150525074816/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/hall-resigns-cowboys-5976/|url-status=live}} He played 23 games, scoring 4 tries and starting the majority of his games in the second-row. In round 10 of [[2013 NRL season]], Hall played his 150th NRL game in the Cowboys' 12–8 loss to the [[Sydney Roosters]]. Later that year, he signed a one-year contract extension with the Cowboys.{{cite web |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/cowboys-lock-in-talent-10654/ |title=Cowboys lock in talent {{!}} NRL {{!}} Zero Tackle |website=zerotackle.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130808055010/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/cowboys-lock-in-talent-10654 |archive-date=2013-08-08}} On 10 October 2014, Hall extended his stay with North Queensland for a further season, re-signing with the club until the end of 2015.{{cite web |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/hall-to-stay-with-the-cowboys-15282/ |title=Hall to stay with the Cowboys {{!}} NRL {{!}} Zero Tackle |website=zerotackle.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141011033637/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/hall-to-stay-with-the-cowboys-15282/ |archive-date=2014-10-11}} On 31 August 2015, Hall announced his retirement from rugby league at the end of the [[2015 NRL season]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/glenn-hall-retires-from-nrl/tabid/10874/newsid/89367/default.aspx|title=Glenn Hall retires from NRL|date=31 August 2015|access-date=31 August 2015|archive-date=3 September 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150903005821/http://www.nrl.com/glenn-hall-retires-from-nrl/tabid/10874/newsid/89367/default.aspx|url-status=live}} On 27 September 2015, Hall played in the [[Townsville Blackhawks]] [[Intrust Super Cup]] Grand Final loss to the [[Ipswich Jets]]. == Post playing == As of 2020, Hall has been in a behind the scenes role at the North Queensland Cowboys.{{Cite web |date=2020-04-26 |title=Sea Eagles Q and A: Glenn Hall |url=https://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2020/04/26/sea-eagles-q-and-a-glenn-hall/ |access-date=2025-07-27 |website=Manly Warringah Sea Eagles |language=en}} ==Achievements and accolades== ===Individual=== '''[[List of North Queensland Cowboys honours#Club awards|North Queensland Cowboys Club Person of the Year]]''':
